---
title: The price of colour
author: ''
date: '2022-06-13'
categories: []
tags: [R, conservation, wildlife trade, colour]
image:
  caption: ''
  focal_point: ''
bibliography: [refs.bib]
link-citations: true
summary: Exploring the role of plumage colour in the global bird trade
output:
  blogdown::html_page:
    toc: true
    number_sections: true
    toc_depth: 1
runtime: shiny
---

# People like colour

* lots of examples of people's love of colour
* what people love is important because it influences what they want to see, protect, and own
* these forces can be at odds with each other

![_Surface temperature is important for small, flat things that don't float in the air at breast height_](/post/2019-10-21/RIMG0902.JPG) 

# Not just about song in songbirds...

![_Too many images and no idea how to use them?_](/post/2019-10-21/FLIR8561.JPG) 

* many songbirds are traded for their song
* but, many are also very colourful!

# How do we even measure colour?

* it's hard to measure colour in a way that is intuitively meaningful to humans
* there are many different components to how we see things, and how we value what we see
* and all of this is going to be very subjective, and probably varies between regions and cultures
* it's also hard to visualise colour, because standard ways of measuring colour are multidimensional

<iframe height="800" width="100%" frameborder="no" src="https://antoinesoetewey.shinyapps.io/statistics-201/"> </iframe>

<br>
